item.title: Machine Learning Knots Tutorial
item.description: This is a introductory Jupyter notebook on machine learning methods. In particular, the examples carried out here aim to get one up to speed with ML concepts such as feed-forward neural networks, long-short term memory networks, decision trees and K-nearest neighbour models in PyTorch and SciKit-Learn.

item.title: WPI-SKCM2 Winter School, Japan 2024
item.description: Presentation given at the WPI-SKCM2 winter school conference @ Hiroshima Univeristy, Japan.

item.title: Spot-a-Knot Game
item.description: Spot a Knot game for teaching the difficulty in discerning topological objects (specifically knots) from one another for the general public featured in Physics World magazine.

item.title: 3D Smectic Liquid Crystals
item.description: During the summer of 2023 I was funded to work on a project concerning the development of 3D smectic liquid crystal simulation in C++ and Python. My work concerned developing methods to constrain tensors to physically realizable states using lagrangian multipliers.

I’m a 1st year PhD maths student based at the University of Edinburgh, jointly supervised by Davide Michielleto & Tudor Dimofte. I work primarily on the intersection between mathematics, computer science and biophysics, using machine learning to investigate problems in knot theory spanning both topological invariants, and the relationship between structure and family of proteins.

Talks

WPI-SKCM2 Winter School, University of Hiroshima, Japan 2024

  • Machine Learning for Knot Invariants on Open and Closed Curves

IBM research, Nairobi, Kenya 2025

  • Deep Learning for Low Dimensional Topology & ML interpretability.

Knots & Proteins, Univeristy of Bologna, Italy 2025

  • Machine learning for Knot Classification

Seminars

Applied Geometry and Topology in Edinburgh

  • Co-organizer for AGATE seminar (summer term 2025)

Conferences & Schools

WPI-SKCM2 Winter School, University of Hiroshima, Japan 2024

Quantum Topology & Hyperbolic Geometry, ICTP VIASM, Vietnam 2025

item.title: Research
item.description:
Deep Learning for Knot Classification

  • Using various deep learning architectures to accurately classify different low-dimensional topologies (specifically knots) and to learn novel relationships between geometry and topology.

Deep Learning for Knot Invariant Relationships

  • Probing conjectured relationships between invariants of knots using data analysis and machine learning methods.

Knot Theoretic Measures for Protein Family Classification

  • Investigating the relationship between hierarchical protein families (CATH) and structural features motivated from geometry and topology.
